channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Volkswagen recortará la mitad de sus modelos ante la caída de las ventas
item.description: El fabricante de coches alemán ha tenido dificultades para competir con las empresas chinas en rápido crecimiento que ofrecen vehículos eléctricos más asequibles y sofisticados.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing, Theresa Rauffmann and Jim Tankersley

media:content.media:credit: Ingmar Nolting para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una fábrica de Volkswagen en Zwickau, Alemania, en 2024. El fabricante de coches es una parte fundamental de la base industrial del país.

item.title: China Launches Reusable Rocket, Clearing a Key Hurdle in Race With SpaceX
item.description: The launch and recovery of the Long March 10B could represent a long-awaited breakthrough for Chinese satellite companies.
item.dc:creator: Selam Gebrekidan and Pei-Lin Wu

media:content.media:credit: Visual China Group, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A Long March 10B rocket blasting off from the Wenchang spaceport in Hainan, China, on Friday.

item.title: Que la ‘Odisea’ se convierta en tu próxima lectura
item.description: La epopeya más famosa del mundo tiene algo que ofrecer a cada lector.
item.dc:creator: A.O. Scott

media:content.media:credit: Jim Stoten

item.title: Rusia capturó Chernóbil. Las cámaras del bosque siguieron filmando
item.description: La región radiactiva en Ucrania se ha convertido en un refugio para la fauna silvestre. Un estudio registró los efectos de la ocupación rusa de 2022 sobre los animales del territorio.
item.dc:creator: Emily Anthes

media:content.media:credit: Sociedad Zoológica de Fráncfort, Reserva de Radiación y Biosfera Ecológica de Chernóbil/Programa Wild Polissia
media:content.media:description: Los investigadores observaron pocos cambios de comportamiento en los linces, mientras que diferentes especies de ciervos modificaron sus patrones de actividad durante la ocupación rusa.

item.title: Contaminated Food, Unwashed Hands: Inside an ICE Detention Facility
item.description: Detainees at the Delaney Hall center in Newark have complained for months about dirty, unhealthy living conditions. Documents echo their claims.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ley, Claire Fahy, Mark Bonamo and Nicole Hong

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Delaney Hall has emerged as a flashpoint in President Trump’s immigration crackdown.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 10, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Houston Residents Mourn Neighbor Killed by ICE
item.description: Residents of Houston’s Magnolia Park neighborhood set up a memorial on the street where an ICE agent fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, who was not the agency’s intended target, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Hundreds of community members held a rally and candlelight vigil for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, an undocumented immigrant from Mexico who was killed after an encounter with federal agents in Houston.

item.title: Indian Diaspora, Now the Largest Foreign-Born Group in Australia, Welcomes Modi
item.description: A large, festive reception for the Indian prime minister in Melbourne belied tensions over migration in the country.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim

media:content.media:credit: Hollie Adams/Reuters
media:content.media:description: People cheering during the Melbourne Meets Modi community event at a stadium in Melbourne, Australia, on Friday.

item.title: Ronny Chieng Tackles Trump's Two Air Force Ones
item.description: “Is that why there’s no gas in the world anymore? Because the president flies with an extra emotional support plane?” Chieng said on “The Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: The Beautiful Truth About Helen of Troy
item.description: She was a legendary beauty, but also so much more.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Mendelsohn

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Sam Whitney/The New York Times

item.title: The World’s Superpowers Are Scrambling for an Edge. It Makes All of Us Less Safe.
item.description: There’s a way for nations to avoid endless trade wars.
item.dc:creator: Pierre-Olivier Gourinchas

item.title: Which Jay-Z Album Is Better? ‘Reasonable Doubt’ vs. ‘The Blueprint’
item.description: Watch the full debate here.
item.dc:creator: Popcast

item.title: 12 People Die in Southern Spain Wildfire
item.description: The blaze broke out in Almería province in Andalusia and prompted a major operation to contain it. Heat waves across Europe this summer have raised the risk of wildfires, forecasters say.
item.dc:creator: Qasim Nauman

item.title: Trump Administration Fires Members of Independent Election Group
item.description: The firings and a resignation render the Election Assistance Commission useless. The moves come as President Trump seeks to impose control over how ballots will be counted in the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green

media:content.media:credit: Chris Carlson/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The White House confirmed that it had dismissed members of the Election Assistance Commission, a bipartisan group tasked with helping states carry out elections.

item.title: Meet Neil, Australia’s Celebrity Seal
item.description: The young elephant seal has gained fans around the world. But the authorities warn he could be in danger of being loved to death.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

item.title: Seal’s Mischievous Antics Draws Crowds and Caution
item.description: Neil the seal has smashed fences and lounged in the middle of the road, drawing crowds of adoring fans. Wildlife experts warn that too much popularity can be dangerous.
item.dc:creator: Jiwoong Hong

item.title: Los ataques de Irán corren el riesgo de reavivar la guerra a gran escala con EE. UU.
item.description: La historia de Irán muestra que el régimen del país ha estado dispuesto a atacar y prolongar conflictos, incluso cuando eso pueda implicar derrotas importantes.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: Buques en el estrecho de Ormuz el miércoles. El tráfico del fin de semana pasado fue de aproximadamente un tercio del nivel anterior a la guerra, que superaba las 100 embarcaciones diarias, repartidos a partes iguales entre los lados iraní y omaní de la vía navegable.

item.title: Quote of the Day: The Man Who Cried ‘Goooooooooal’
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, July 10, 2026.

item.title: Through Sickness, They Remained ‘Healthy With Each Other’
item.description: Ron Zofnat and Trevor Jacobson dated for three months before Zofnat was diagnosed with cancer. They then embarked on a “dual track” life.
item.dc:creator: Alyson Krueger

item.title: He Annoyed Me, Which Is Exactly What I Needed in a Man
item.description: My anxiety made it too hard to date. Then I met a perfectly irritating guy on Bumble.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Singer

item.title: Love and Hockey Kept Them Moving, Often Together
item.description: Lane Radovan and Nate Clurman, a professional hockey player, first met as high school freshmen in 2013, but didn’t become official until 2019.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: A Rough Introduction, a Fast Friendship and One Very Cozy Cat
item.description: Jonathan Samuel Masci was a bit standoffish during his first encounter with Nadia Tiffany Gakou, but their relationship flourished when he finally understood the assignment.
item.dc:creator: Ivy Manners

item.title: A Wedding That Recaptured Their Carefree Camp Days
item.description: Jackie Miller and Matt Hochberg were born in the same hospital and attended the same preschool, but first met as teens at summer camp.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: What Graham Platner’s Implosion Reveals About the Democratic Party
item.description: Lisa Lerer, a New York Times national political correspondent, explains how recent allegations against the Senate candidate Graham Platner and his subsequent withdrawal from Maine’s primary race have reignited a longstanding conflict between the progressive and establishment wings of the Democratic Party.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, Stephanie Swart, Nour Idriss and Lauren Pruitt

item.title: Man Charged in National Guard Shooting Is Hospitalized for Refusing Food
item.description: The “dire” condition of the suspect, who has pleaded not guilty to murder and other charges, was “self-inflicted,” a judge said.
item.dc:creator: Sejal Govindarao

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Law enforcement responding to the shooting, near Farragut Square, in Washington last year.

item.title: Esta estrella se tragó a un planeta y eso fue apenas el aperitivo
item.description: El planeta engullido a 1300 años luz de distancia pudo haber sido lanzado hacia la estrella por su vecina, una enana marrón que los científicos creen que también será devorada.
item.dc:creator: Becky Ferreira

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 10, 2026
item.description: Willa Angel Chen Miller returns to open our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Stuart Palley for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Please remember this is not a full-contact crossword puzzle.

item.title: What We Know About the ICE Shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo
item.description: Mr. Araujo was a father, a husband and a business owner who had moved to the United States 35 years ago from Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A makeshift memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo near the site where he was killed in Houston.

item.title: The U.S. and Iran Are Fighting Again. Is the Cease-Fire Over?
item.description: Eric Schmitt, a New York Times National Security correspondent, analyzes recent skirmishes between the U.S. and Iran and whether they threaten ongoing negotiations for a peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Gilad Thaler, Jon Miller, Rafaela Balster, Stephanie Swart, Jon Hazell and Whitney Shefte
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Bombing Iran, Then a Tentative Accord Didn’t Work. Does Trump Have a Plan C?
item.description: The administration appears to be reverting to an all-stick, no-carrot approach. But it has yet to answer why it believes economic warfare and bombing will yield a different result this time.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Monday. The United States has bombed more than 170 Iranian military targets over two nights this week as a cease-fire unraveled.

item.title: New Air Force One Lacks Defensive Countermeasures of Previous Model, Officials Say
item.description: Experts said the lack of such capabilities poses a potential risk when the president travels overseas. The White House defended the aircraft’s safety.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Eric Lipton, Adam Goldman, Eric Schmitt and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The new presidential jet before its maiden flight as Air Force One last week.

item.title: A Top OpenAI Executive, Fidji Simo, Steps Down
item.description: Ms. Simo, who was Sam Altman’s second in command before taking a medical leave in April, will become a part-time adviser to the company.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz

media:content.media:credit: Stuart Isett/Fortune, via Shutterstock
media:content.media:description: Fidji Simo became OpenAI’s first chief executive of applications in March 2025.

item.title: ‘This One Is Going in the Forever Rotation’
item.description: That’s a reader describing this tomato and cottage cheese salad, a protein-packed take on a favorite bagel sandwich.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: Mark Weinberg for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Monica Pierini.
media:content.media:description: Amelia Rampe’s tomato and cottage cheese salad.

item.title: Mexican Man Fatally Shot by ICE Wasn’t the Intended Target
item.description: Officials say agents believed a passenger resembled one suspect, but the encounter quickly escalated into a fatal shooting.
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval and Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Hundreds of community members held a rally and candlelight vigil for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, an undocumented immigrant from Mexico who was killed after an encounter with federal agents in Houston.

item.title: Iranian Attacks on Jordan Highlight Kingdom’s Difficult Position
item.description: Iran’s military said it fired missiles at a Jordanian air base used by American forces. Jordan said it had intercepted missiles entering its air space.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Planet Labs Pbc, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: A satellite image shows Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Al Azraq, Jordan.

item.title: Bonnie Tyler, Singer of ‘Total Eclipse of the Heart,’ Dies at Age 75
item.description: The pop singer Bonnie Tyler, known for her ’80s hits like “Total Eclipse of the Heart” and “Holding Out for a Hero,” died in a hospital in Portugal from an illness on Wednesday, according to her official Facebook account.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:credit: Adrian Greer Michael Short/Fairfax Media, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Bonnie Tyler in 1983. Her biggest hit, “Total Eclipse of the Heart,” became a cultural mainstay that has 1.1 billion streams on Spotify and 1.3 billion views on YouTube.

item.title: N.Y.C. Council Speaker Turns Up Pressure on Schools Chief Over Contracts
item.description: Julie Menin, the speaker, said she had been given excuses for why contracts had not been provided. The mayor’s office called her request “extremely laborious.”
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Julie Menin, the speaker of the New York City Council, has criticized the city’s Department of Education over its handling of contracts.

item.title: Officer Charged in Deaths of Police Dogs After Leaving Them in S.U.V. for Hours
item.description: A 4-year-old Belgian Malinois and a 6-year-old springer spaniel were left alone in May for seven hours without relief from the heat, according to prosecutors in South Jersey.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

item.title: New York Sues Companies Over ‘Forever Chemicals’
item.description: The lawsuit claims that the companies, including 3M and DuPont, contaminated the environment and engaged in fraud by withholding information from the public about the harms of the chemicals, known as PFAS.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron and Hilary Howard

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Letitia James, the New York attorney general, said that chemical companies operating in the state “knowingly sold toxic products.”

item.title: Is Trump Following Viktor Orban’s Playbook?
item.description: Will Trump ultimately come to the same grief as Viktor Orban? On “Interesting Times,” columnist Ross Douthat asks writer Rod Dreher about the parallels between America and Hungary under Orban’s former regime.

item.title: Prosecutors Show New Evidence in Charlie Kirk Case
item.description: Also, China and Russia seek to inflame the data center debate. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Kim Raff for The New York Times

item.title: Palm Beach Airport in Florida Is Renamed for Trump
item.description: The name change, approved earlier this year, took effect on Thursday, the Federal Aviation Administration said. New signage with President Trump’s name is expected roll out in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

media:content.media:credit: Saul Martinez/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A sign welcomed visitors to President Donald J. Trump International Airport, formerly Palm Beach International Airport, in Florida on Thursday.

item.title: When ‘Hero’ Doesn’t Mean What You Think It Does
item.description: It’s time for a crash course in “The Odyssey,” before Christopher Nolan’s new adaptation hits theaters. The classicist and author Madeline Miller explains how this 3,000-year-old poem keeps getting reinvented, and why Odysseus is so compelling — and so flawed. Video by Amy Pearl and Patricia Sulbarán/The New York Times.

item.title: As Workers Shore Up Midtown Building, Developer Reassures Investors
item.description: Crews installed “temporary shoring” on more floors in the building, and the developer of the project has reassured investors that the project will be completed on time.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri, Claire Fahy, Maureen Farrell and Caitlyn Freeman

media:content.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: At the building that was at risk of partially collapsing in Midtown Manhattan, crews are still at work to shore up the safety of the building.

item.title: In Boston, Fans With Roots Across a Continent Turned Out for Morocco
item.description: While Morocco did not advance to the semifinals, a large, diverse African diaspora in New England took pride in the team, which they adopted as their own.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell

media:content.media:credit: Brian Snyder/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Moroccan fans rallied on Boston Common on Wednesday, before the match in Foxborough, Mass., on Thursday.

item.title: Are We Living Under Therapeutic Totalitarianism?
item.description: We’re in an age of “therapeutic totalitarianism,” the writer Rod Dreher argues, “where they give you everything you want, all the drugs, the porn, the sex, the entertainment.” All that’s required in return? “You just have to surrender your humanity,” he says on “Interesting Times.”

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo Is the Jalen Brunson of Pop
item.description: On “Cannonball,” Wesley Morris and the pop music journalist Caryn Ganz find the right metaphor to describe Olivia Rodrigo’s place in pop music.
item.dc:creator: Gavy Contreras, Lauren Pruitt, Andrew Smith and Felice Leon

item.title: After Several Days, Ayatollah Khamenei’s Funeral Comes to an End
item.description: A huge crowd surrounded Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s coffin in his hometown, Mashhad, on Thursday, the final day of his funeral procession. The supreme leader’s body was transported to the Imam Reza Shrine, where it will be buried.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Atta Kenare/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Mourners gathered for the funeral procession for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s slain supreme leader, in Mashhad, Iran, on Thursday.

item.title: Are We Becoming ‘Weimar America’?
item.description: America is at risk of extreme political upheaval, the conservative writer Rod Dreher argues on “Interesting Times.” “It may not be Hitler 2.0, it may not be Stalin 2.0, it might be something all American, but it’s not going to be what we’re used to,” he says.

item.title: Suspect in Charlie Kirk Killing Admitted to the Crime in Text Messages, Prosecutors Say
item.description: In messages that prosecutors say were sent to his romantic partner, Tyler Robinson admitted he was the person who shot Charlie Kirk during an event at a Utah university.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Spenser Heaps
media:content.media:description: A photograph, which prosecutors say shows a text message exchange between Tyler Robinson and Lance Twiggs, is shown during a preliminary hearing at court in Provo, Utah, on Thursday.
